name: @faker-js/faker (Fake Data Generation for JS/TS)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); faker-js Community (community fork of faker.js)

agent_readiness.notes: Comprehensive fake data generation. import { faker } from '@faker-js/faker'. faker.person.firstName(). faker.person.lastName(). faker.internet.email(). faker.internet.url(). faker.phone.number(). faker.location.city(). faker.location.country(). faker.location.latitude(). faker.date.past(). faker.date.between({from, to}). faker.finance.amount(). faker.finance.creditCardNumber(). faker.commerce.productName(). faker.lorem.paragraph(). faker.image.url(). faker.string.uuid(). faker.number.int({min, max}). faker.helpers.arrayElement([...]). faker.helpers.fake('{{person.firstName}} {{person.lastName}}'). Locales: faker.locale = 'de'. Seeding: faker.seed(123). faker.helpers.multiple(factory, {count: 10}).

gotchas: Locale data size can be large when including all locales
faker.seed() must be called before generating to ensure reproducibility
Generated data is not cryptographically random — don't use for security tokens
Some locale-specific data may be incomplete (faker.location varies by locale)
best_when: Test data generation for unit, integration, and E2E tests
Seeding databases with realistic fake data
API mocking with realistic fake responses
avoid_when: Production systems — clearly mark generated data as fake
summary: @faker-js/faker generates comprehensive fake data. faker.person.firstName(), faker.internet.email(), faker.location.city(), faker.date.past(), faker.helpers.arrayElement(). Locale support. Seeding with faker.seed(). MIT by faker-js community. Type-annotated. Best for test data generation and database seeding. Use @faker-js/faker not the unmaintained faker.js. Seed for reproducibility. Not for security tokens.
